content: If we borrow the linguistic pedagogy framing of reflexion as both a vocabulary concept and a deliberate learning practice from high school English curricula, we can design LLM self-reflection mechanisms that mirror how human students learn to master new reasoning patterns through repeated vocabulary practice, active reflection on past errors, and explicit reinforcement of correct conceptual mappings. This turns LLM self-reflection from an abstract agent optimization step into a structured, iterative learning routine aligned with how humans build durable reflective skill.
